name: Buttermilk Doughnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg until light and foamy in a medium-sized bowl.
2. Add the sugar, beating to combine.
3. Stir in the buttermilk, vanilla extract, flour mixture (baking powder, baking soda, salt, ginger, and mace), and mix until just combined.
4. Chill the dough for 1 hour.
5. Preheat the deep fryer to 375°F (190°C).
6. Prepare the glaze by combining the powdered sugar and warm milk in a bowl; whisk until smooth.
7. Pat the chilled dough into a sheet that is about 3/4 inch thick on a lightly floured surface.
8. Cut out doughnuts using a floured doughnut cutter.
9. Carefully lower the doughnuts into the hot oil, frying them for 2-2 1/2 minutes until golden brown.
10. Remove from the oil and drain excess fat on a paper towel.
11. Brush the warm tops of the doughnuts with glaze; place glazed sides up on a rack to set.
Yields: 8 Doughnuts
